Question: When will General ticket sales start?
Answer: General Admission ticket sales for Devcon India will launch May 20. This initial ticket launch offers our lowest price for general admission this year. Tickets can only be purchased with ETH on Ethereum Mainnet (L1). Stay tuned for updates as we get closer to this date!

Question: Can I purchase tickets outside of Waves?
Answer:
No — General Admission tickets are only available during waves. However, we _will_ have limited free & discounted tickets available on [our ticket shop](https://devcon.org/tickets/store) throughout the year.

We will also be featuring a Patron Ticket this year; this ticket can be bought outside of waves to directly support the Scholarship Program.

Question: Tickets are sold out - how can I attend?
Answer: Subscribe for updates or reminders, and follow our social channels for announcements about additional ticket releases.

Question: Will there be opportunities to obtain discounted tickets?
Answer:
Yes! There will be multiple ways to obtain discounted tickets this year:

- **Local Discounts** — We will have limited tickets reserved for Indian residents.
- **Community Discounts** — This will consist of groups like Protocol Guild members, OSS Contributors and more.
- **Applications** — This will include Builder Discounts, Student Discounts, and Youth Tickets for those under 18.

Question: I'm a student, can I attend Devcon?
Answer:
Yes — we offer discounted tickets for Indian and International students to join us at Devcon 8 India. Students should fill out our [application form](https://devcon.org/en/form/student-application/) and if approved, we’ll send you a voucher link to purchase your ticket. Indian and International Student tickets are priced at $25 and $99 respectively. **There will be 2 rounds of application review:** - **Round 1 – Apply by June 12, responses sent by July 15.** - **Round 2** – **Apply by August 14, responses sent by Sept. 7.**
**All applicants should meet the following criteria:** - Currently enrolled in an accredited university degree program (Bachelor, Master, or PhD) - Studying fields such as computer science, engineering, mathematics, economics, law, governance, public policy, or other relevant disciplines - Students contributing to research, open-source projects, or academic work related to blockchain, cryptography, governance, or digital public infrastructure - Students involved in university research groups, blockchain clubs, policy initiatives, or developer communities - Students with a demonstrated interest in Ethereum and open technologies Please note: Short-term courses, bootcamps, and online-only programs are not eligible.

Question: If I am accepted for a discount after buying a full-priced ticket, can I get a refund of the difference?
Answer:
Yes — You can:

1. **Cancel** your original order using the link in your confirmation email, then **redeem** the new Voucher Code, OR
2. Reach out to support@devcon.org.

Question: Can I purchase tickets with crypto?
Answer: Yes! You will be able to choose between Credit Card or Crypto to pay for your ticket. Orders paid using crypto will receive a discount off the total cost.

Question: How can I cancel my order?
Answer: You can cancel your order by returning to your Order page found in your original confirmation email & clicking "Cancel Order."

Question: What if I only need to cancel some tickets on an order with multiple?
Answer: You can request a partial cancellation by contacting our support team at support@devcon.org.

Question: What are the policies towards Refunds & Transfers?
Answer: Refunds will be permitted until the start of Devcon. General Admission tickets are anonymous and therefore transferrable. Discounted tickets are non-transferrable.

Question: If I buy a ticket, and then I am accepted to Speak, can I get a refund for the original ticket I purchased?
Answer: Yes, if you are accepted as a speaker, you can request a refund for your original ticket purchase.

Question: I plan on bringing my child to Devcon with me. Do they need a ticket?
Answer: If your child is between the ages of 3–17, they will need a Youth ticket. Children under the age of 3 do not need a ticket. A Youth Ticket is only valid for those under 18.

Question: I need a Visa invitation Letter. How can I obtain one?
Answer: Visa invitation letters will be available for ticket holders closer to the event date. Details will be shared with you via email.

Question: Can I transfer my ticket to someone else?
Answer: General Admission tickets are anonymous and transferrable. Name/email can be changed using the link in your original Order Confirmation email. **Discounted tickets are non-transferrable**.

Question: Can I buy tickets on behalf of someone else?
Answer: General Admission tickets can be bought for someone else, but all discounts must be applied for & claimed by the individual who is attending.

Question: Is there a ticket purchase limit?
Answer: Yes — there is a limit of 2 tickets per order.

Question: Are there discounts for Indian residents?
Answer: Yes — we will have limited discounted tickets reserved for Indian residents. You can verify your residency using your Aadhaar Card with Self.xyz on [our ticket shop](https://devcon.org/en/tickets/store/).

Question: Are there grants or scholarships available for those who can't afford a ticket?
Answer: We are currently offering a range of discounts to keep Devcon accessible, including Community, Builder, and Student tickets. We're also working with local universities and Ethereum communities to ensure we're getting tickets into the hands of the most deserving people on the ground.

Question: How do I apply for a Builder discount?
Answer: Builder discount applications will open in June. [<u>Follow us on X</u>](https://x.com/EFDevcon) to be one of the first to know when the applications launch.

Question: Are there group or team discounts available?
Answer: While we do not offer group or team tickets, we offer plenty of discounts to individual applicants. You may also consider becoming a Supporter in our Supporter Program (TBA soon)!

Question: I'm from an academic background, how can I / my community get involved in Devcon?
Answer: Please [apply for the Student Discount here](https://devcon.org/en/form/student-application/)! If you have further questions, feel free to reach out to us at support@devcon.org.

Question: Which cryptocurrencies are accepted for payment?
Answer: We accept ETH, USDC and USDT on Ethereum, Arbitrum, Base and Optimism. L2 payments made with stablecoins on our ticket shop benefit from gas sponsored transactions.

Question: Which fiat payment methods are accepted?
Answer:
All major debit and credit card providers are accepted and you can checkout using Google and/or Apple Pay.

**Please note:** Payments made via Fiat will be processed on our **Pretix Store**.

Question: I'm having trouble completing my payment – what should I do?
Answer: Follow any on-screen steps and ensure you have enough funds to complete the transaction. If this isn’t working, reach out to our Support team at [<u>support@devcon.org</u>](mailto:support@devcon.org) – we’ll be happy to help.

Question: Do I need to bring ID to registration to collect my wristband/badge?
Answer: All Discounted ticket types (Locals, Builder, Student, Youth) **<u>must show ID</u>** at Registration. All other ticket types can enter with just their QR code.

Question: What does my ticket include access to?
Answer: All 4-day tickets include full conference access, catering, and hot drinks.

Question: Is the event accessible for attendees with disabilities?
What is the dress code / are there any venue rules I should be aware of?
Answer: While there is no specific dress code for Devcon, we will uphold a "No shirt, no shoes, no entry" rule for entry. No weapons, drugs, drones, or animals are permitted inside the venue. Smoking or chewing of tobacco is prohibited.

Question: Will sessions be recorded or streamed for those who can't attend in person?
Answer: Yes, sessions will be recorded & streamed on our [YouTube channel](https://www.youtube.com/@EthereumFoundation) for those who cannot attend Devcon.

Question: Do I need a visa to travel to India?
Answer: Yes – most foreign nationals need a visa to enter India. To be safe, [<u>apply for an e-Visa online</u>](https://indianvisaonline.gov.in/evisa/) at least two weeks before traveling – though the process is usually fast. It is recommended that your passport be valid for at least six months from the arrival date.

Question: What airport(s) should I fly into?
Answer: The closest airport to Mumbai is the Chhatrapati Shivaji Maharaj International Airport (BOM). It’s approximately 10–12km from BKC (about 30–45 minutes by taxi).

Question: Are there recommended hotels or accommodation near the venue?
Answer: Stay tuned! We’ll be releasing our City Guide containing useful travel info and recommendations with our Global Ticket launch on May 20.

Question: When will I get my ticket?
Answer: You can add your ticket to your Apple or Google Wallet immediately after placing your order. We will send a PDF containing your ticket QR a few days prior to Devcon.

Question: Who can apply for a discounted ticket?
Answer: Discounted tickets are available through applications for students, builders, and ecosystem contributors. Each category has its own eligibility criteria and pricing. Applications are reviewed and tickets are limited.
